mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing cycle that reduces discount rates for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.03 over 2-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth above 150k jobs signals robust labor demand, driving corporate earnings optimism and positive S&P 500 returns within 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in high-payroll scenarios fall below 0% despite PAYEMS > 150k.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts above 1.3 million units indicate strong consumer demand, boosting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in high-housing scenarios fall below 0% despite HOUST > 1300.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in GDP and real consumer spending signals strong aggregate demand, creating positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days through earnings momentum.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns show negative or flat performance when both GDPC1 and RSAFS are rising.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production above 100 indicates improving supply-side capacity, reducing inflationary pressures and generating positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in high-industrial scenarios fall below 0% despite INDPRO > 100.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation above 3% triggers central bank tightening, increasing real interest rates and creating negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns in high-inflation scenarios rise above 0% despite CPIAUCSL > 3.

mechanism_narrative: Low unemployment combined with rising rates signals a tightening labor market, increasing wage pressures and creating negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in tight-labor scenarios rise above 0% despite UNRATE < 4 and FEDFUNDS > 4.5.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent declines in GDP and housing starts signal weakening demand, creating negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days through earnings pessimism.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns show positive performance when both GDPC1 and HOUST are declining.
